howItWorks.intro: This calculator scores your home's disaster resilience (0-100) across five categories and identifies the highest-ROI upgrade based on current weaknesses.
rules: Each category (roof, windows, power, flood, fire) is scored 1-4 and weighted equally for the composite score
The top recommended upgrade targets your lowest-scoring category for maximum resilience improvement
Insurance savings are estimated at 5-15% per major upgrade based on typical insurer discounts
ROI calculation includes both insurance premium reduction and reduced expected loss from damage avoidance
Whole-home generators and impact windows typically offer the best combination of resilience and insurance savings
howItWorks.outro: Resilience scores are relative estimates. Actual insurance savings vary by insurer, state, and policy. Many upgrades also increase home value by 1-5%. Contact your insurer before upgrading to confirm specific discount eligibility.

faq.question: What is a home resilience score?
faq.answer: A home resilience score measures how well your property can withstand and recover from natural disasters including hurricanes, wildfires, floods, and power outages. Scores factor in structural materials, protective features, backup systems, and defensible space. Higher scores correlate with lower insurance costs and damage risk.

faq.question: What home upgrades have the best ROI for resilience?
faq.answer: Impact windows typically offer the best ROI: $15,000-25,000 installed with 15-30% insurance savings plus increased home value. Roof upgrades (impact-rated shingles or metal) cost $10,000-20,000 and save 10-25% on insurance. Whole-home generators ($8,000-15,000) prevent food spoilage, pipe freezing, and habitability losses.

faq.question: Do resilience upgrades increase home value?
faq.answer: Yes. Impact windows add 1-3% to home value, metal roofs add 1-6%, whole-home generators add 3-5%, and a fortified roof-to-wall connection system can add value in hurricane-prone areas. These features are increasingly important selling points as climate risks grow.

faq.question: How much can I save on insurance with resilience upgrades?
faq.answer: In hurricane-prone states, a full wind mitigation package (impact windows, hip roof, roof-to-wall clips) can save 30-50% on windstorm premiums. Individual upgrades typically save 5-15% each. In fire zones, defensible space and Class A roofing can save 10-20% on fire premiums.

faq.question: What is the FORTIFIED Home designation?
faq.answer: FORTIFIED Home is a program by the Insurance Institute for Business & Home Safety (IBHS) that certifies homes built or retrofitted to exceed code for wind and hail resistance. FORTIFIED Gold designation can reduce insurance premiums by 30-55% in participating states like Alabama, Mississippi, and South Carolina.

sections.title: What Is the Home Resilience Calculator?
sections.body: The Home Resilience Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your home value, roof type, window protection, you get instant results including resilience score, grade, top recommended upgrade. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting resilience score right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your home value and need to find the right resilience score. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Home Value ($)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your home value — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: roof type, window protection, backup power, flood preparedness, fire preparedness, annual insurance premium. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your resilience score, grade, top recommended upgrade. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your home value before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both resilience score and grade — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to get accurate results.
If you're unsure about your annual insurance premium, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
